title: Magisterial probe ordered into Ballia custodial death allegation; SP team to visit village
content: The Samajwadi Party said a 14-member delegation, constituted on the directions of party president Akhilesh Yadav, will visit Gaighat village on July 17 to express condolences to the bereaved family

title: Delhi HC directs daily medical monitoring of Sonam Wangchuk amid indefinite hunger strike
content: The petition argued that the government could not remain a passive spectator, contending that failure to intervene could amount to “abetment of suicide” under Section 108 of the Bharatiya Nyaya Sanhita, 2023

title: Supreme Court asks CBSE not to introduce third language in Class 9
content: “Union of India, please do not have a third language in Class 9. Pressure [of the Board exams] starts for children in Class 8,” Justice Nagarathna said

title: Why are rival Trinamool Congress factions competing to hold the July 21 Martyrs’ Day Rally? | Explained
content: For more than three decades, the Martyrs’ Day rally in West Bengal has been synonymous with Mamata Banerjee. This year, rival Trinamool factions and the Congress are competing to claim its political legacy

title: Kanwariyas vandalise pickup after 'contact' with kanwar in Uttar Pradesh’s Muzaffarnagar
content: According to Station House Officer (SHO) Manvendra Singh Bhati, the pickup vehicle allegedly brushed against one of the 'kanwars', triggering anger among the pilgrims who allegedly vandalised the vehicle.

title: Protest in Shivamogga over allowing students of private university and engineering course to apply for Agriculture Officer post
content: KEA allowed students who had obtained a B.Sc. (Agriculture) degree from Rai Technology University, a private university, and those with a B.E. degree in biotechnology to apply for posts of Agriculture Officer and Assistant AO

title: Women's quota, Delimitation Bills will be passed in Monsoon Session: Athawale
content: We have a two-thirds majority. Women's Reservation Bill and Delimitation Bill will be passed, says Union Minister Ramdas Athawale

title: Sonam Wangchuk’s decision to join CJP protest organic, says wife Gitanjali Angmo
content: Sonam Wangchuk wanted to ensure that the CJP’s cause is not sabotaged by “nefarious” elements, and the movement does not go astray, says his wife Gitanjali J. Angmo

title: Trinamool MP Samirul Islam calls on family who was pushed into Bangladesh
content: Four Bengali migrants from Birbhum who were detained by Delhi Police last year and pushed into Bangladesh were brought back to India on July 8, 2026 after a year-long legal battle. It was Samirul Islam who took up the cause of the migrant workers and provided help to the family

title: Sonam Wangchuk loses over 9 kg, weakens significantly on 19th day of hunger strike: CJP
content: Sonam Wangchuk remains under constant medical supervision, the CJP said in a statement, adding that he is still ‘mentally alert’, even as he has weakened significantly

title: What is the row over Karur ‘temple land’? | Explained
content: When Tamil Nadu CM Vijay visited Karur on July 10, activist T.R. Ramesh accused the TVK government and the HR&CE Department of handing over the lands in question —valued at around ₹25,000 crore— to “encroachers"

title: What are the gains from the India-U.K. trade deal? | Explained
content: Zero tariffs. Cheaper whisky and British cars. Major benefits for Indian workers in the U.K. The India-U.K. trade deal could reshape business on both sides. Here’s the complete picture

title: Raut urges Fadnavis to take part in 'Ram Raksha Andolan' of Sena (UBT) in Nagpur
content: Sena (UBT) head and former State CM Uddhav Thackeray had earlier said that he would take part in the Ram Raksha Andolan organised by the party in Nagpur, the hometown of CM Fadnavis, on Saturday (July 16).

title: Nagpur Cyber Police register FIR against content creators over alleged defamatory campaign targeting Nitin Gadkari
content: The legal action stems from highly contested online posts regarding the government's Ethanol-blended (E20) fuel policy

title: 12-year-old boy dies after collapsing in school in Bengaluru; family gets CCTV camera footage one month later, alleges negligence
content: The CCTV camera footage reportedly shows the boy informing his teacher that he was feeling unwell before returning to the classroom. Moments later, he allegedly collapsed on his bench

title: Kannada social media influencer booked for sexual harassment in Bengaluru
content: According to the complaint, the alleged incident took place during the promotion of a pub, where Manju had reportedly consumed alcohol and behaved inappropriately with a female server

title: Supreme Court to hear appeal against ban on animated film Mahaprabhu Jagannath for not being true to ‘Skanda Purana’
content: In an oral mentioning, senior advocate Devadatt Kamat, representing the film’s makers, Ele Animations, said the movie was due for release on July 17 in 300-odd theatres across the country

title: ED searches 13 locations in alleged terror-funding and illegal infiltration case
content: The case is linked to a First Information Report (FIR) registered by the Anti-Terror Squad (ATS) of Uttar Pradesh. The searches were carried out in Delhi, Uttar Pradesh, Haryana, West Bengal, and Maharashtra

title: Farmers stage motorcycle rally to protest against proposed India-U.S. trade deal
content: Farmers behind the ‘Desh Bachao Morcha’ urge the Central government to ‘prioritise the interests of farmers, workers, small traders, small industries and India’s economic and food sovereignty, and refrain from implementing any agreement that goes against the interests of the people’

title: No shortage of fertilisers in NTR district, says Collector
content: Lakshmisha says the government follows a transparent fertiliser distribution system through the A.P. Agribusiness Information Management System app, and fertilisers are given to farmers based on online land records

title: Two hurt as fishing boat capsizes near Visakhapatnam Fishing Harbour; all seven survive
content: The vessel overturned in strong currents and wedged between rocks near the Fishing Harbour; two injured fishermen are being treated at the KGH, less than a fortnight after six others were lost off Gangavaram

title: Kuppam: a granite quarry hub, now emerging as a peacock paradise
content: Residents point out various reasons for the development, such as suitable climatic conditions, the protective nature of local farmers, etc; peacocks became an integral part of the landscape in rural and semi-urban areas; the birds were often seen near village tanks, and farm ponds during summers

title: Kerala Agricultural University releases five new high-yielding crop varieties
content: New chilli, brahmi, vetiver, ginger and turmeric varieties promise higher yields, better quality and improved farm income

title: T.N. will take appropriate steps to get Cauvery water for farmers: Agriculture Minister
content: Vinoth was responding to views expressed by Karnataka Water Resources Minister Ramalinga Reddy on the State’s inability to release Cauvery water to TamilNadu due to insufficient rain and low storage levels in its reservoirs

title: Hyderabad pharma firm executives donate ₹2 crore to TTD scheme in Tirumala
content: Biophore India Pharmaceuticals chief executive officer Jagadish and chief strategy officer Manik Reddy Pullagurla each contributed ₹1 crore; TTD chairman B.R. Naidu received the demand drafts at his Tirumala Camp Office

title: Interpretation of sexual offences should not be limited only to physical act: NCW chief
content: “While interpreting sexual offences, the focus should not be limited only to the physical act. Equal importance must also be given to the survivor’s dignity, her consent, the fear she experienced, and the psychological trauma caused by the incident,” Ms. Rahatkar said.

title: Devotees throng ISKCON Jagannath temple in Vijayawada for Nava Yauvana darshan
content: The first public darshan of Lord Jagannath, Lord Balabhadra and Goddess Subhadra after the 15-day Anavasara period drew devotees to the Krishnalanka temple; the chariot procession sets off from Sitara Grounds at 2 p.m. on July 16

title: Provident Fund interest credited to all 34 crore accounts on July 15
content: The Union Finance Ministry had recently approved the recommendation of the EPFO’s Central Board of Trustees to provide interest at the rate of 8.25%; a senior EPFO official said the credited interest will be visible to all members when they open passbook for 2025-26.

title: Hurdles before Assam-Nagaland border oil exploration | Explained
content: Extremist and traditional tribe-based organisations in Nagaland do not want oil majors to resume oil exploration and extraction until the interstate border dispute and the “Indo-Naga political issue” are resolved

title: KLS GIT inaugurates ‘factory on campus’ in Belagavi
content: Apart from giving students access to industry-grade tools, the factory would provide them valuable experience before they graduate

title: Nearly half of 844 million chronic kidney disease cases remain undiagnosed worldwide: Lancet
content: Researchers call for routine urine testing to detect chronic kidney disease earlier, warning that delayed diagnosis is putting millions at risk of kidney failure and premature death

title: Another COVID-19 death reported in A.P., three more test positive
content: The death of 66-year-old man has been attributed to comorbidities, and not the COVID-19 infection alone

title: BJP asks Congress to clarify stand on Saifuddin Soz’s “internal autonomy” remarks
content: Veteran Congress leader Saifuddin Soz advocated for the restoration of Jammu & Kashmir’s Statehood and Article 370, as well as “internal autonomy”.

title: Kerala’s government hospitals face a shortage of specialist doctors
content: Over 280 specialist doctor posts remain vacant in government hospitals, according to RTI data; more than 50 hospitals with delivery points lack adequate teams of gynaecologists, paediatricians and anaesthetists; doctors’ association calls for urgent recruitment, creation of more posts and faster promotions

title: Bengaluru: Apartment residents seek builder accountability, parking norms in new Bill
content: One stakeholder called for the Bill to prescribe clear timelines for conducting association elections and define the tenure of office-bearers

title: Bidadi project: Hours after Karnataka Chief Minister announces review panel, govt. issues final land notification for acquiring 4,944.49 acres across four villages
content: This is the biggest tranche of land notified yet. The first set pertained to only 518.45 acres in three villages

title: Abnormal heatwave conditions to persist across Tamil Nadu till July 17; rainfall set to improve later this week
content: Officials of the RMC said the weak monsoon circulation over the Southern Peninsular region and El Niño-related atmospheric conditions led to limited cloud formation and an unusual heat spell in July

title: Proposed apartment law may not be perfect, but it is a good start; Greater Bengaluru Development Minister Krishna Byre Gowda seeks feedback
content: Mr. Gowda pointed out that the existing laws were enacted over 50 years ago, and the city had grown by leaps and bounds since then.

title: Apartment owners in Bengaluru get briefing on new law for property owners from Karnataka CM
content: He informed that the proposed Karnataka Apartment (Ownership and Management) Act, 2026 has been drafted to protect the interests of homebuyers, and ensure complete ownership rights

title: Latur coaching owner paid ₹5 lakh to procure NEET questions from NTA paper-setter, says CBI
content: The CBI has found that 111 of these questions allegedly match with the questions in the NTA’s master question sets prepared for NEET (UG) 2026

title: India-U.K. DCC not applicable to Indians working in the U.K. before July 15
content: DCC came into effect on July 15, 2026 alongside the India-U.K. Comprehensive Economic and Trade Agreement (CETA)

title: Gujarat announces rehabilitation package for Surat flood-affected traders
content: The package provides lump-sum financial assistance to street vendors, handcart operators and owners of small and large cabins to help resume business operations in the affected areas

title: Madras High Court nullifies registration of land belonging to Palani math
content: A Division Bench of Justices C.V. Karthikeyan and R. Sakthivel allowed the appeal preferred by the mutt against a Single Bench order that had directed the Sub-Registrar to register the sale deed if the document was otherwise in order

title: On the Grand Road of Puri
content: The Jagannatha temple in the city in Odisha is an impressive place of worship, and the grand festival of the deity is incomparable

title: 'Every life is precious': Delhi HC urges Centre to regularly examine Wangchuk's health
content: Hearing the a PIL over fasting activist's dwindling health, the high court noted that ​​"life is precious" and asked both the governments to provide, if needed, medical aid, to the Ladakh native. Doctors have warned that Wangchuk may soon enter a potentially "alarming" phase involving organ damage. Wangchuk has reportedly lost more than 9kgs since beginning his indefinite hunger strike.

title: 'We will kill Trump': Tehran puts up gigantic billboard depicting US president in coffin
content: A large billboard showing Donald Trump in a coffin appeared in Tehran's Enghelab Square. This display coincided with renewed military strikes between the United States and Iran. US forces conducted strikes targeting Iranian command centers and air defense systems. Iranian authorities reported a missile strike near a cancer hospital in Ahvaz. Damage was also reported at an industrial facility in Qeshm following an enemy attack.

title: India's first hydrogen train set for launch: All you need to know
content: India will soon launch its first hydrogen fuel cell train on Friday. Prime Minister Narendra Modi will flag off the train in Haryana. This train generates its own electricity onboard using hydrogen fuel. The project establishes India's first integrated hydrogen railway ecosystem. This initiative marks a significant step towards cleaner transport solutions.

title: Passport lost, stranded alone in Europe: Survival of Md Imran, India's upcoming GM
content: At 16, Indian chess prodigy Md Imran was stranded in Slovakia after a bus carrying his passport, visas and belongings left without him. Despite the ordeal, financial struggles and lack of institutional support, he played on to earn two Grandmaster norms. Now on the verge of becoming India's newest GM, Imran urgently awaits reissuance of his US visa to join his scholarship at UTRGV before August 23.

title: Judge blocks Trump's visa curbs for foreign researchers, trust & safety experts
content: A US federal judge has blocked an immigration policy targeting foreign researchers. This policy could deny visas or deport professionals working on misinformation. The judge ruled the policy likely violates the First Amendment's free speech protections. The decision affects individuals linked to US-based organizations combating digital hate. This ruling prevents the government from enforcing the policy during ongoing legal proceedings.

title: US Fed enlists ex-RBI guv Rajan, 2 other Indians for massive policy overhaul
content: Former RBI governor Raghuram Rajan will help shape US monetary policy. He joins a Federal Reserve task force reviewing its policy framework. Rajan will examine balance sheet policy alongside other experts. Other panels will assess data, productivity, and inflation frameworks. These initiatives aim to improve the central bank's future policy approach.

title: Messi, space & Argentina's relentless pressure: How final 10 mins broke England
content: Messi produced two assists in the last minutes as Argentina came from behind to beat England 2-1 on Wednesday (local time) and book a place in Sunday's World Cup final against Spain. Enzo Fernandez equalised in the 85th minute before Lautaro Martinez headed home Messi's cross in the second minute of stoppage time to complete another Argentina comeback.

title: Jeff Bezos' ex-wife MacKenzie Scott donates to US youth mental health non-profit
content: MacKenzie Scott has donated twenty million dollars to Active Minds. This significant contribution is the largest in the organization's history. Active Minds works with young people to improve mental health awareness and reduce stigma. The unrestricted funds will expand programs and support youth leaders. Scott continues her extensive charitable giving after selling Amazon shares.

title: Hyderabad school terminates teacher who allegedly gave 'Kalma' recitation as homework
content: A Hyderabad private school terminated a teacher after assigning religious homework. Parents of a Hindu student protested the school's instruction to recite Islamic prayers. The school issued a termination letter and permanently disqualified the teacher from future employment. BJP spokesperson Shehzad Poonawalla claimed his intervention led to the teacher's removal. The school has not publicly commented beyond issuing the termination letter.

title: Workers enter ‘forever layoff’ era as firms cut jobs while hiring for AI
content: Microsoft and other tech giants are implementing frequent workforce reductions.  These companies are simultaneously increasing investments in artificial intelligence technology.  Many firms are reorganizing operations to adapt to the evolving technological landscape.  This trend reflects a broader industry shift towards continuous workforce adjustments.  Employees face uncertainty as businesses navigate AI integration and productivity demands.

title: JPMorgan CEO: Anthropic's Mythos AI is like giving ballistic missiles to individuals
content: JPMorgan CEO Jamie Dimon expressed concerns regarding advanced AI models. He warned that powerful AI tools like Anthropic's Mythos pose significant risks. US authorities are closely monitoring these advanced artificial intelligence capabilities. The bank is actively testing these AI models for cybersecurity vulnerabilities. Traditional cybersecurity practices remain crucial alongside AI advancements.

title: Karnataka govt proposes new apartment bill: Why it matters for flat owners
content: Karnataka plans a new law to govern apartment ownership and management. This legislation will replace outdated acts from nineteen seventy-two. The proposed bill clarifies land and common area ownership for apartment owners. It also introduces mandatory structural checks for older buildings. A two-tier dispute resolution system will be established under the new law.

title: Jon Gruden on Antonio Brown: 'He finished everything' and NFL's future
content: Jon Gruden discussed his biggest coaching regret, the Antonio Brown situation with the Raiders. He expressed concern over modern NFL offenses, which he feels lack communication. ​During a recent appearance on Cam Heyward's Not Just Football podcast, the Super Bowl winning coach reflected on one of the biggest disappointments of his career and shared why he believes today's NFL is moving in the wrong direction.

title: Roman Reigns vs Seth Rollins net worth 2026: Salary, brand deals compared
content: Roman Reigns and Seth Rollins will clash at SummerSlam 2026 in a highly anticipated match. Reigns boasts an estimated net worth of twenty million dollars and earns fifteen million annually. Rollins holds an estimated net worth between nine and twelve million dollars, earning seven million. Both wrestlers have lucrative WWE contracts and significant merchandise sales contributing to their wealth.

title: Erling Haaland's 'five girlfriends' comment: The viral twist explained
content: Erling Haaland's old "five girlfriends" comment has resurfaced, surprising fans years after it first made headlines. While many initially thought the Norway striker was talking about his dating life, he was actually referring to the five footballs he kept after scoring hat-tricks. The lighthearted remark highlighted his obsession with scoring goals long before he became a global superstar, Manchester City's leading striker and one of football's highest-paid players.

title: Army chief visits crucial Siliguri Corridor - why Chicken's Neck area is critical for India
content: India is actively enhancing infrastructure to secure its vital land bridge connecting the mainland to Northeast states. Railways plans a new underground line, and highway expansion is accelerating through central agencies. Land has been transferred to border security forces for improved surveillance and troop movement. The Army Chief reviewed security dynamics and modernization initiatives in the Eastern Command. He also outlined his vision for an agile and future-ready Indian Army.

title: Will gold head to Rs 1.45 lakh/10 gm & silver reach Rs 2.27 lakh per kg? Check outlook
content: Gold and silver price prediction today: Gold and silver prices are showing signs of recovery after a phase of weakness, says Abhilash Koikkara, Head - Forex & Commodities, Nuvama Professional Clients Group.

title: 17-Year-Old Leads 300 US Navy Cadets Before School, Nears Naval Officer Dream
content: Riddhi Chauhan, a 17-year-old Indian-American student from Queens, has secured admission to the Naval Academy Preparatory School, taking a step towards becoming a commissioned US Navy officer. As the Battalion Commanding Officer of her school’s NJROTC unit, she leads nearly 300 cadets through drills, training and mentoring activities before classes begin. Her journey includes multiple leadership roles, academic achievements and the development of her unit’s first SeaPerch underwater robot.

title: SC upholds Class I heirs' preferential right over inherited agricultural land
content: The Supreme Court has ruled that Section 22 of the Hindu Succession Act, 1956 applies to inherited agricultural land, reaffirming that Class I heirs enjoy a preferential right to purchase the share of another co-heir before it is transferred to a third party.

title: 'Mossad or CIA': Vance hints at Epstein-Israeli intel link; admits Trump admin 'screwed up'
content: JD Vance admitted that the Trump administration mishandled the communication surrounding the release of the Jeffrey Epstein files, blaming much of the confusion on former Attorney General Pam Bondi's public remarks. He also defended Donald Trump against allegations of trying to suppress the documents and suggested that intelligence agencies like Mossad or CIA had connections to Epstein.

title: Gill's chase at Edgbaston showed why India already have Kohli's ODI heir
content: Shubman Gill's unbeaten 80 against England in the first ODI at Edgbaston was a lesson in calculated ODI batting rather than outright aggression. On a seamer-friendly surface, he absorbed pressure, rebuilt after India's early setbacks and accelerated at the right moments to steer the chase. Even cramps couldn't derail his composure, reinforcing why Gill, who is still uncertain for the second ODI in Cardiff, is rapidly becoming India's premier chase specialist, much like Virat Kohli.

title: 'Avoid deploying Indian seafarers': Centre tells ship owners as Iran war reignites
content: In a post on X, the maritime regulator said the decision had been taken to protect the safety, security and welfare of Indian seafarers as hostilities in the region continue to intensify. The DGMA noted that several merchant vessels, including Mombasa B, Al Bahyah, GFS Galaxy, MT WEDYAN and AL REKAYYAT, have recently come under attack, highlighting the growing risks to commercial shipping and crews transiting the area.

title: Wedding band didn't show up for baraat; consumer court orders Rs 39,000 payout
content: A wedding band operator was fined Rs 39,000 for failing to appear at a wedding. The district consumer commission found him guilty of deficiency in service and unfair trade practice. He accepted Rs 4,200 advance but never sent the promised brass band. The groom's family had to arrange a replacement band at the last minute. The commission ordered a refund, compensation, and litigation costs.

title: At least 500 Rohingya feared dead after two boats capsize in Bay of Bengal
content: Two boats carrying Rohingya people capsized in the Bay of Bengal. At least 500 individuals are feared dead after these tragic incidents. The boats departed from Myanmar's Rakhine state and Bangladesh refugee camps. These perilous sea crossings occur during the dangerous monsoon season. The international community is urged to increase support for Rohingya refugees.

title: A R Rahman receives standing ovation at Wolf Trap concert with national symphony orchestra
content: Music maestro A R Rahman captivated a packed audience during his concert with the National Symphony Orchestra at Wolf Trap, earning a standing ovation.

title: 'Has severely hit projects': Govt tightens Isro exit rules as 120 scientists quit in a year
content: Isro's Department of Space has introduced new rules for scientist resignations. These new regulations aim to prevent departures from key projects like Gaganyaan. The department now requires central directors to refer all such requests to DoS. This move reverses a 2020 decision that empowered centre directors to accept resignations. Isro acknowledges departures but states it is prepared to manage the situation.

title: NPCIL denies nuclear systems breach after Kudankulam data leak report
content: India's Nuclear Power Corporation stated a data breach involves conventional plant facilities. The reported leaked files pertain only to non-nuclear safety systems. These documents were prepared by Reliance Infrastructure Ltd. for common services. The breach does not compromise nuclear security or reactor core systems. The affected information relates to plant units under construction.

title: Bengaluru law student dies after man stabs her for rejecting his brother's marriage proposal
content: A law student died after being stabbed by the brother of a man whose marriage proposal she rejected. The accused attacked the victim at her residence after she ended their relationship. He allegedly used a knife during the altercation, inflicting serious chest and back wounds. Local residents apprehended the attacker immediately after the violent incident occurred. Police have arrested both brothers and are continuing their thorough investigation.

title: 'They better behave': Trump says Iran wants settlement amid fresh strikes
content: President Trump warned Iran to behave if it desires negotiations with Washington. US forces conducted new military strikes targeting Iranian command centers and air defense systems. These strikes aimed to degrade Iran's ability to threaten commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz. Iranian authorities reported a missile attack near a cancer hospital in Ahvaz. Damage was also reported at an industrial facility in Qeshm following an enemy attack.

title: Skipped land acquisition hearing? Supreme Court says you can't claim denial later
content: The Supreme Court upheld land acquisition for a Jaipur Metro rail depot. Landowners failed to appear for hearings and pursue their objections. The court ruled their conduct barred claims of denied hearings under the 1894 Act. Authorities largely followed required processes for land acquisition. Landowners can still seek higher compensation for their acquired property.

title: Moonlighting software engineer earns Rs 7 lakh, yet fears AI
content: A 29-year-old software engineer earning Rs 7 lakh a month has sparked discussions about financial anxiety, work pressure and the meaning of success. Working two IT jobs and nearly 16 hours a day, Rohit says his drive comes from family responsibilities and fears around AI-led job changes. Despite owning assets and having substantial savings, he continues chasing ambitious financial goals. His story highlights the importance of financial planning, adaptability, skill development and maintaining work-life balance.

title: FIFA WC: Messi shines as Argentina pull off stunner against England to reach final
content: Argentina clinched a dramatic 2-1 win against England on Wednesday, ensuring their place in the FIFA World Cup Final. The defending champions staged a remarkable comeback after trailing for a significant part of the match, as England's lead crumbled in the final moments. Next up for Argentina is a face-off against Spain in the grand finale this Sunday.

title: How Spain's triangles dismantled France's World Cup dream
content: An uncharacteristically banal France met their match in an uncannily beautiful Spain. A France entered the examination hall with a class-topper’s swagger and suddenly looked in an incremental confusion. A Spain, perhaps a little detached from all this pre-match chaos, turned ecstatic with the joy of passing the test with seamless proficiency.

title: Are banks closed today for Rath Yatra and Harela? Check state-wise list
content: Bank branches will be closed in Odisha, Uttarakhand, and Manipur on July 16. These closures are for Ratha Yatra and the Harela festival celebrations. Customers can still use online and mobile banking services for transactions. Other regional holidays are scheduled later this month across various states. Regular banking operations will resume on the next working day.

title: SUV at 140kmph crashes on Mumbai-Goa highway; 3 of family and driver dead
content: A newly married couple's family faced tragedy returning from Goa. Their SUV crashed into a parked dumper truck on the Mumbai-Goa highway. Four family members and the driver died in the devastating accident. The groom sustained severe injuries and is hospitalized for treatment. Police are investigating the dangerously parked truck and the speeding SUV.

title: Man charged with murder after Indian woman stabbed to death at home in London
content: A 24-year-old Indian woman was fatally stabbed at her London home. A man has been charged with murder and attempted murder in connection with the incident. The victim's family alleges the attack was racially motivated and seeks government assistance. They had sold land to fund her education and future aspirations in the UK. British Sikh groups express concern over rising race-based attacks targeting vulnerable individuals.

title: 'Not in good shape but ...': Sonam Wangchuk refuses to end fast despite health concerns
content: Sonam Wangchuk continues his hunger strike for nineteen days, stating his health is not critical. He urged supporters to join a Parliament march on July 20 instead of asking him to break his fast. Wangchuk believes his protest sends a message about government accountability and the need for action. His health has been monitored, and medical tests show stable results for his condition.

title: Explained: What is Argentina's 'Las Malvinas' Falkland Islands controversy?
content: Argentina's World Cup semifinal victory over England has ignited a fresh controversy.  Midfielder Giovani Lo Celso displayed a banner claiming the Falkland Islands are Argentine.  FIFA rules prohibit political messages during football matches, potentially leading to disciplinary action.  The Falkland Islands have been a subject of dispute between Argentina and the United Kingdom for decades.  Argentina's Foreign Ministry recently reaffirmed its claim over the islands in an official communiqué.

title: Messi's reaction after Argentina players find England goalkeeper's secret penalty notes
content: Lionel Messi and Argentina teammates sparked a viral moment after their World Cup semi-final victory over England, as they inspected Jordan Pickford's penalty notes taped to his water bottle. The England goalkeeper's preparations for a shootout went unused after Lautaro Martínez's stoppage-time winner sealed a 2-1 comeback. Messi's amused reaction quickly became one of the tournament's most talked-about moments.

title: New Microsoft Security Head Replaces Top Execs In AI Overhaul: Report
content: Hayete Gallot who took over Microsoft's security business in February has replaced several senior executives as part of the company's AI restructuring, according to a report by The Information. Microsoft is the largest cybersecurity software seller in the world.

title: PM Modi Pushes To Cut Import Reliance To Shield India From Shocks
content: Prime Minister's office ordered key ministries to identify categories of goods in which import dependence is high, and can be replaced by locally made products, according to officials familiar with the matter.

title: US, China Lead AI Race. Where India Stands And What's Really At Stake
content: The AI race isn't about prestige. Experts say AI is becoming critical infrastructure-just like electricity, telecom networks or cloud computing.

title: Delhi Cop Installing Speed Monitoring Device Gets Killed By Speeding Car
content: The incident took place at around 6 pm at the Singhu border in outer north Delhi when the policeman was installing a speed monitoring machine near a traffic booth.

title: Hyderabad Parents Claim School Told Student To Learn 'Kalma' For Homework
content: According to the family, their six-year-old child returned home with a homework diary containing instructions to read the 'Kalma' and 'Fateh'.

title: Russia Seeks More Fuel From India As Ukraine Attacks Refineries: Report
content: India is the biggest buyer of Russian seaborne crude oil, making Moscow's bid to secure Indian gasoline an unusual reversal in the countries' energy trade relationship.

title: 'Woman Can't Claim Maintenance From Step-Son After Relief From Real Son': Allahabad Court
content: Justice Laxmi Kant Shukla, in his judgement passed on Tuesday, said that after receiving maintenance from her real son, the mother is no longer incapable of maintaining herself and hence not entitled to any subsequent maintenance order in her favour.

title: 'People Will Take Oxygen Shots': Bombay High Court On Shrinking Green Cover
content: "It is a loss for this area. As it is, Bombay has so little oxygen that the day will not be far when people will carry oxygen cylinders to take oxygen shots," Bombay High Court remarked.

title: Indian Mother, 24, Stabbed To Death At UK Home, Family Calls It 'Hate Crime'
content: Kaur's family has alleged that the assailant entered her bedroom and attacked her with a sharp-edged weapon in what they believed was a racist attack.

title: Rs 1,200-Crore Nutrition System, Empty Plates: Why Madhya Pradesh's Children Are Still Going Hungry
content: An NDTV ground investigation across Bhopal, Sagar, Vidisha and Agar-Malwa found that take-home ration supplies had remained disrupted for periods ranging from one to three months at several centres.

title: PM Modi To Launch India's First Hydrogen-Powered Train From Haryana Tomorrow
content: India joins a select group of countries, including Germany, Japan, China, and the US, that are exploring the hydrogen for cleaner rail transportation.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE: Jordan Says It Shot Down 8 Iranian Missiles
content: US-Iran War Live Updates: A fifth of all traded crude oil and natural gas passed through the Strait of Hormuz before the war, when it was open to all without tolls.

title: PM Modi's Rs 25,000 Crore Bet: Ganga, Varuna Corridors To Transform Kashi
content: The projects come at a time when Varanasi is witnessing a surge in visitors. The city attracts nearly 15 crore tourists and pilgrims every year.

title: Stock Market LIVE Updates, Sensex Today: Sensex Jumps 200 Points Higher Amid Renewed US-Iran Tensions
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty LIVE Updates: Crude oil prices rise for 4th session amid escalating US-Iran war; Brent crude at $85.28.

title: Amid War, Volunteers Try To Save Gaza's Past, One Artefact At A Time
content: Armed with paintbrushes and makeshift scanners, Gaza volunteers race against airstrikes to save 5,000 years of history from the rubble.

title: "Human Rights Disaster": Detainees At ICE's Texas Facility Allege Abuse
content: 90% of detainees interviewed at ICE's Fort Bliss facility said they had either personally been assaulted by the staff or had seen others physically abused, as per a report.

title: Why Europe's Deadly Heatwave Is More Than Just A Summer Crisis
content: A persistent high-pressure system, combined with warmer-than-average sea surface temperatures, has trapped an immense mass of hot air over Europe, driving extreme temperatures across the continent.

title: US Ropes In Ex-RBI Governor Raghuram Rajan, 2 Other Indians For Key Fed Roles
content: Rajan will serve on the balance sheet policy task force to examine the costs, benefits, and institutional implications of the Federal Reserve's current balance sheet regime, including its asset holdings and their role in implementing monetary policy.

title: Pune Cops Visit Rajasthan Temple to Verify Siya-Chetan Secret Marriage Claim
content: Pune Police visited Khatu Shyam Temple to verify reports of a secret marriage between Ketan Agarwal murder accused Siya Goyal and Chetan Chowdhury

title: Mumbai-Ahmedabad Bullet Train Bridge Unaffected By Rains, Clarify Officials
content: Rejecting media reports claiming that a temporary bridge erected for the construction of the main bridge was washed away, officials clarified that the affected structure was only a temporary access arrangement
